mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 10%, labor market deterioration signals severe demand destruction, causing equity markets to price in prolonged earnings declines and producing negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 10.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with a p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital expenditure growth, which amplifies corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series; the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime where CPI exceeds 250, persistent price pressures force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and generating negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes noise during high inflation periods; out-of-sample classification accuracy drops below 50% when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which correlates with higher corporate earnings and yields positive forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative or near zero across all rolling 3-year windows; industrial production growth shows no statistical relationship with subsequent equity returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 0.5%, economic momentum stalls, triggering recession fears that depress equity valuations and produce negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where GDPC1 growth falls below 0.5%, or the signal predictive accuracy is indistinguishable from random chance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment indicates a strengthening labor market without overheating, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction effect is statistically insignificant; joint movements in payrolls and unemployment fail to predict forward returns better than a baseline model over 5-year out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: During stagflationary conditions where CPI exceeds 255, weak retail sales growth signals eroding purchasing power, forcing equity markets to discount future earnings and generating negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power or flips direction when CPIAUCSL exceeds 255; out-of-sample classification accuracy falls below 45% in stagflationary regimes over any 3-year window.
